# Teresa Torres 2026 Roadmap: AI-Powered Discovery and the Discovery Habits Toolbox

**Published:** 2026-01-14 | **Author:** Teresa Torres | **Site:** producttalk.org

## Summary

Teresa Torres published her 2026 strategic roadmap for Product Talk, announcing two parallel tracks: (1) AI teaching tools for learning discovery habits, and (2) AI automation tools for doing discovery work. The most significant announcement is her expanded partnership with **Vistaly** to deliver AI-powered interview analysis and OST generation directly inside the tool teams already use. She also retired live deep-dive cohorts in favor of on-demand courses and the new **Discovery Habits Toolbox** (a leader-coaching program). The 2026 roadmap is framed explicitly as "a roadmap of experiments, not features" - Torres eating her own dog food by applying continuous discovery habits to her own business decisions.

Key strategic moves:
- **Discovery Habits Toolbox** replaces live cohorts for corporate clients: leaders get a coaching hub with tools to spread discovery habits inside their org.
- **AI Interview Coach** (already launched) enables personalized feedback on interview quality - extending deliberate practice to every student.
- **Vistaly partnership**: Upload 3 interviews for the same outcome, AI extracts key moments and opportunities from each, synthesizes across interviews, and generates a *draft* OST. Torres is emphatic: this is AI-assisted synthesis, not one-click fake OSTs. "A draft OST you actually refine is better than a perfect process you never get to."
- Torres is personally spending 60% of her time doing real engineering work (Claude Code) to build these tools.

Critical framing: Torres is concerned that AI is re-prioritizing delivery over discovery industry-wide. "AI is making it easier and faster to build. And so we're once again putting even more emphasis on delivery. And we're forgetting about, hey, maybe we should be asking, is this the right thing to build?" She sees AI-powered discovery tools as the course correction.

## Key Quotations / Statistics

- "Continuous Discovery Habits turns five this year! Over 135,000 people have bought the book. And thousands of teams are putting the habits into practice."
- "Don't just outsource your discovery work, even though it is rapidly becoming just now possible. Do both. Develop your discovery skills. Let AI do some, you do some, figure out the best balance for your team."
- "A draft OST you actually refine is better than a perfect process you never get to."
- On AI teaching vs automation: "I'll be splitting my time between building AI teaching tools that help you learn the discovery habits and building AI tools that help you automate many of the discovery habits."

## Annotations for stinger-forge

- **Relevant to `guides/03-interview-cadence.md`:** Reinforces the weekly cadence benchmark and adds context that even Torres is now using AI for synthesis to lower the recruiting/scheduling tax.
- **Relevant to `guides/02-opportunity-solution-tree.md`:** The Vistaly AI-OST workflow (3 interviews in -> draft OST out) is the leading-edge 2026 tooling story. Stinger should mention Vistaly as the recommended tooling integration.
- **Critical directive source:** "Develop your discovery skills. Let AI do some" aligns with the Bee's mandate - the Bee coaches the team, AI augments the work. This is the correct posture for 2026.
- **Potential contradiction:** Torres' concern about AI re-prioritizing delivery deserves a note in `guides/00-principles.md` - teams adopting AI-build tools without AI-discovery tools will regress to feature factories.
